description: Browser agent benchmark — 15 test cases on bot-friendly public sites

benchmarks.name: Eventbrite Free Event RSVP
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://www.eventbrite.com/d/online/free--events/
Find the first free online AI or tech event. Click on it.
Fill the registration form with Name: Amit Paliwal, Email: test@amit.com.
Submit RSVP. Screenshot the confirmation page.
Save event-details.json with event name, date, URL, confirmation status.

config.expected: event-details.json contains event name, date, and URL fields

benchmarks.notes: Registration may require HITL for bot-detection. Browser must be used.

benchmarks.name: Wikipedia Research + PDF Summary
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multi-agent_system
Extract the Introduction and Applications sections.
Screenshot the page. Save summary.md with 10 key facts.
Create report.pdf from the markdown using pandoc.
benchmarks.notes: Wikipedia has zero bot protection. PDF via pandoc (in sandbox).

benchmarks.name: Hacker News Jobs CSV
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://news.ycombinator.com/jobs
Extract all visible job listings: company name, role, URL.
Save to jobs.csv with columns: company, role, url, scraped_date.
Screenshot the page. Create top5.md with your recommended picks.
benchmarks.notes: HN has no bot protection, simple HTML.

benchmarks.name: GitHub Trending Repos
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://github.com/trending?since=daily&spoken_language_code=en
Extract top 10 trending repos: name, stars today, description, URL.
Save trending.json. Create alert.md if any repo has >500 stars today.
Screenshot the trending page.
benchmarks.notes: GitHub trending is static HTML, always accessible.

benchmarks.name: arXiv Paper Fetch + Summary
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://arxiv.org/search/?searchtype=all&query=AI+agent+orchestration&start=0
Find the first 3 papers. Extract title, authors, abstract, PDF link.
Save papers.json. For each paper write a 3-sentence summary in summaries.md.
Screenshot the search results page.
benchmarks.notes: arXiv is academic, no bot protection.

benchmarks.name: Product Hunt AI Products
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://www.producthunt.com/topics/artificial-intelligence
Extract today's top 5 AI products: name, tagline, upvotes, URL.
Save to products.json. Create competitive-analysis.md comparing them
against AI Partner on: pricing, features, target user.
Screenshot the page.
benchmarks.notes: Product Hunt uses JS — browser_get_content handles it.

benchmarks.name: Yahoo Finance Stock Data
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/RELIANCE.NS/
Extract current price, day high, day low, 52-week range, volume.
Save stock-data.json. Generate a price summary as chart.png using Python matplotlib.
Write analysis.md: is price above 52-week average? Buy/Hold/Sell signal.
benchmarks.notes: Yahoo Finance replaces NSE (which blocks bots). No Cloudflare.

benchmarks.name: Eventbrite Meetup + Calendar
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://www.eventbrite.com/d/online/free--events/?q=AI+meetup
Find the next 3 free AI or tech meetups. Extract name, date, time, URL, description.
Save meetups.json. Create calendar.ics with all 3 events in standard iCalendar format.
Screenshot the results page.
benchmarks.notes: Eventbrite replaces Meetup.com (which requires login). Public listings work.

benchmarks.name: Old Reddit Thread Summarizer
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://old.reddit.com/r/artificial/top/?t=week
Find the top post this week. Open it. Extract the post + top 10 comments.
Save thread.json with post title, content, comments array.
Create digest.md: 5 key insights from the discussion.
Screenshot the thread.
benchmarks.notes: old.reddit.com is plain HTML — far more reliable than new Reddit.

benchmarks.name: DuckDuckGo Multi-Source Research
benchmarks.prompt:
Search DuckDuckGo for 'AI agent frameworks comparison 2026' at https://duckduckgo.com
Click the first 3 results. Extract key content from each page.
Save sources.json with URL + 200-word summary per source.
Create research-report.md with synthesized findings across all 3 sources.
benchmarks.notes: DuckDuckGo itself works. Per-site content varies.

benchmarks.name: Books to Scrape Price Monitor
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://books.toscrape.com/catalogue/category/books/mystery_3/index.html
Extract prices of first 10 mystery books: title, price, rating, availability.
Save price-snapshot.json with timestamp.
If any book costs under £10, write alert.txt with those book details.
Screenshot the results.
benchmarks.notes: books.toscrape.com is purpose-built for scraping. Always works.

benchmarks.name: Hacker News Morning Digest
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://news.ycombinator.com
Extract top 10 stories: title, URL, points, comment count.
Save hn-digest.json. Create morning-brief.md formatted as a daily newsletter
with headline, one-line summary per story, and a 'Story of the Day' pick.
Screenshot the front page.
benchmarks.notes: HN is simple HTML. Should be a reliable pass.

benchmarks.name: GitHub Public Profile Scrape
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://github.com/torvalds
Extract name, bio, location, public repos count, top pinned repositories.
For each pinned repo: name, description, stars, language.
Save profile.json. Write outreach-draft.md: a personalized message based on their work.
Screenshot the profile.
benchmarks.notes: GitHub public profiles need no login. Replaces LinkedIn.

benchmarks.name: YouTube Video Research + Notes
benchmarks.prompt:
Go to https://www.youtube.com/results?search_query=AI+agent+demo+2026
Find the top 3 videos. Extract title, channel, view count (if visible), URL.
Save videos.json. For each video write a predicted summary based on title/description.
Create watch-list.md ranking them by relevance to AI agent development.
benchmarks.notes: YouTube search page is accessible. View counts may need JS.

benchmarks.name: Multi-Site Daily Brief (Hero Demo)
benchmarks.prompt:
You are my research assistant. Without interruption:
1. Scrape GitHub trending (https://github.com/trending) — top 5 AI repos
2. Scrape HN front page (https://news.ycombinator.com) — top 3 AI stories
3. Scrape arXiv (https://arxiv.org/list/cs.AI/recent) — latest 3 AI papers
Combine all into daily-brief.json with sections: repos, news, papers.
Create daily-brief.md as a formatted newsletter. Screenshot each site.
Total files expected: daily-brief.json, daily-brief.md, github.png, hn.png, arxiv.png
benchmarks.notes: Hero demo. GitHub + HN + arXiv all accessible. Run this last.
